items.title: Kyowa Kirin 重掌羅卡替尼單抗的研發和商業化項目，展現出致力解決異位性皮膚炎高度未獲滿足醫療需求的堅定承諾
items.author: Na
items.description: Kyowa Kirin announced it has terminated its collaboration with Amgen on rocatinlimab development and will assume full global control of the program, including regulatory submissions planned for H1 2026. The decision stems from Amgen's strategic portfolio prioritization. Rocatinlimab, a novel T-cell rebalancing therapy targeting OX40 receptors, showed positive Phase 3 results for moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis in trials published in The Lancet, meeting all primary and key secondary endpoints.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Amgen's termination of the partnership reflects strategic portfolio prioritization rather than negative clinical data. The company will continue manufacturing rocatinlimab, maintaining a business relationship. This is a strategic reallocation decision rather than a failure, warranting neutral sentiment.

items.title: Kyowa Kirin recupera el control del programa de desarrollo y comercialización de rocatinlimab, demostrando un firme compromiso para abordar la elevada necesidad médica no cubierta en la dermatitis atópica
items.author: Na
items.description: Kyowa Kirin has regained full global control of rocatinlimab development and commercialization from Amgen due to Amgen's portfolio prioritization. The anti-OX40 monoclonal antibody for moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis showed positive Phase 3 results in late 2025, meeting all primary and secondary endpoints. Regulatory submission in the US is planned for H1 2026, with Amgen continuing as the manufacturer.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Amgen's decision to return rocatinlimab reflects portfolio prioritization rather than negative data. The company will continue manufacturing the drug, maintaining a revenue stream while reallocating resources to higher-priority programs. This is a strategic reallocation rather than a failure.

items.title: Kyowa Kirin Recupera Controle do Programa de Desenvolvimento e Comercialização do Rocatinlimab, Demonstrando Forte Compromisso para Abordar a Alta Necessidade Médica Não Atendida na Dermatite Atópica
items.author: Na
items.description: Kyowa Kirin has terminated its collaboration with Amgen and assumed full control of the rocatinlimab development and commercialization program for atopic dermatitis treatment. The move follows Amgen's strategic portfolio prioritization. Phase 3 trial results published in The Lancet demonstrated that rocatinlimab met primary and secondary endpoints, with regulatory submission planned for H1 2026. Amgen will continue manufacturing the drug.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Amgen's termination of the collaboration reflects strategic portfolio prioritization rather than negative trial results. The company will continue manufacturing rocatinlimab, maintaining a business relationship while redirecting resources to other priorities.

items.title: Kyowa Kirin to regain control of rocatinlimab development, commercialization
items.description: Kyowa Kirin (KYKOF) announced the termination of the current rocatinlimab development and commercialization collaboration with Amgen. Kyowa Kirin will regain control of the global rocatinlimab program, including regulatory filings and future commercialization. This business decision is the result of a strategic portfolio prioritization by Amgen (AMGN). The companies are initiating a smooth and orderly transition of the program, with a focus on ensuring continuity for participants currently enrol
